['"Summary of the Phishing and Attempted Stealing Incident on Binance."
 
Fellow Binancians,

On Mar 7, UTC 14:58-14:59, within this 2 minute period, the VIA/BTC market experienced abnormal trading activity. Our automatic risk management system was triggered, and all withdrawals were halted immediately.

This was part of a large scale phishing and stealing attempt.  

So far: All funds are safe and no funds have been stolen.

The hackers accumulated user account credentials over a long period of time. The earliest phishing attack seems to have dated back to early Jan. However it was around Feb 22, where a heavy concentration of phishing attacks were seen using unicode domains, looking very much like ', {'type': 'link', 'text': 'binance.com'}, ', with the only difference being 2 dots at the bottom of 2 characters. Many users fell for these traps and phishing attempts. After acquiring these user accounts, the hacker then simply created a trading API key for each account but took no further actions, until yesterday.

Yesterday, within the aforementioned 2 minute period, the hackers used the API keys, placed a large number of market buys on the VIA/BTC market, pushing the price high, while 31 pre-deposited accounts were there selling VIA at the top. This was an attempt to move the BTC from the phished accounts to the 31 accounts. Withdrawal requests were then attempted from these accounts immediately afterwards.

However, as withdrawals were already automatically disabled by our risk management system, none of the withdrawals successfully went out. Additionally, the VIA coins deposited by the hackers were also frozen. Not only did the hacker not steal any coins out, their own coins have also been withheld.

The hackers were well organized. They were patient enough to not take any immediate action, and waited for the most opportune moment to act. They also selected VIA, a coin with smaller liquidity, to maximize their own gains.

After a thorough security check by Binance, we resumed withdrawals. Trading functionality was never affected. There are still some users whose accounts where phished by these hackers and their BTC were used to buy VIA or other coins. Unfortunately, those trades did not execute against any of the hackers’ accounts as counterpart. As such, we are not in a position to reverse those trades. We again advise all traders to take special precaution to secure their account credentials.

Protecting our traders is and has always been our highest priority.

Thanks for your support!

- Binance Team']

['Tips :  ', {'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#StopLoss'}, " : 

It's very hard to give a specific STOP-LOSS in Crypto because markets are very VOLATILE. Some traders (myself too) hate using STOP-LOSSES cause most of the time we enter a coin, the coin drops for a re-test, HIT our STOP-LOSS and then goes back UP to ATH probably LOL. Slowly you will be able to understand the right circumstances where and when to use or not to use STOP-LOSSES.

5% Stop-Losses are worthless in Crypto, you would get HIT every time.
10% Stop-Losses are pretty good.
15% Stop-Losses are good cause they give you lots of margin so you do not get HIT, but if you get HIT that sucks.
NO STOP-LOSS if you know what you doing and believe in your trade.

Note :  Always keep BTC chart on a screen and follow it. You know what happens when BTC drops. Everything Drops. So if your STOP-LOSS got hit cause BTC dropt, well that's not as bad as the coin dropping itself for its own reasons."]

It's very hard to give a specific STOP-LOSS in Crypto because markets are very VOLATILE. 5% Stop-Losses are worthless in Crypto, you would get HIT every time.
10% Stop-Losses are pretty good.
15% Stop-Losses are good cause they give you lots of margin so you do not get HIT, but if you get HIT that sucks.
NO STOP-LOSS if you know what you doing and believe in your trade.

# Always Use Stop loss, As BTC movements are uncertain and don't follow TA only, Whales do manipulate it on short Terms.

# Dont Use more than 20-30% of your Bitmex balance in one trade. 

# Try using Lvrg less than 25x in any condition. Risk Reward Ratio.

# Try to stay updated with BTC price while you are in trade or Leave PC only after setting Stop loss and Sell Order. If away try to be updated with our channel.

# However few Calls have hit stop losses yet in our group, Still we should never be overconfident for any trade on Bitmex.

# Keep updating Stop loss in Profit so you dont miss Profit but keep a distance. If stop loss triggered in Profit accept it or if Trend is confirmed rebuy with signalled Stop loss. Keep a ratio for stop loss with your Profit. If you are expecting 50% Profit in a trade stop loss should not be more than 15-20%. With Time you will learn it.

# If you are expectig Big Profits in Bitmex, Try to learn fundamentals of charts and patterns I share so you can become self dependent and can scalp yourself while there is no signal and you are free to trade. Start Practice with lower amount. Learn & Earn.

# Discipline = Profit.

P.S. Trade Less Trade Well Earn More. 😎"]

# As Market is not Clear yet. I would still ask you to sell 50-60% amount at first target and rest near second or third depending on coin. But use stop loss above 4-5% of your buy point after coin hit first target.

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': '3 Rules of Setting Stop Losses

Rule #1: Don’t let emotions be the reason you move your stop.

Like your initial stop loss, your stop adjustments should be predetermined before you put your trade on. Don’t let panic get in the way!


Rule #2: Do trail your stop.

Trailing you stop means moving it in the direction of a winning trade. This locks in profits and manages your risk if you add more units to your open position.

Rule #3: Don’t widen your stop.

Increasing your stop only increases your risk and the amount you will lose. If the market hits your planned stop then your trade is done. Take the hit and move on to the next opportunity.

Widening your stop is basically like not having a stop at all and it doesn’t make any sense so to do it! Never widen your stop!

These rules are pretty easy to understand and should be followed religiously especially rule number 3!'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Types of Breakouts'}, '

', {'type': 'bold', 'text': ' When trading breakouts in trading, it is important to realize that there are two main types:

1. Continuation breakouts
2. Reversal breakouts

Breakouts are significant because they indicate a change in the supply and demand of the chart you are trading
This change in sentiment can cause extensive moves that provide excellent opportunities for you to grab some profit.'}]

['👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆👆
', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Sometimes when there is an extensive move in one direction the market will often take a breather.

This occurs when buyers and sellers pause to see what they should do next.

As a result, you will see a period of range-bound movement called consolidation.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'If traders decide that the initial trend was the right decision, and continue to push the price in the same direction, the result is a continuation breakout. Just think of it as a “continuation” of the initial trend…. You’re so smart!'}, '']
[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Reversal breakouts start off the same way as continuation breakouts in the fact that after a long trend, there tends to be a pause or consolidation'}]

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'The only difference is that after this consolidation, forex traders decide that the trend is exhausted and push the price in the opposite or “reverse” direction.\n\nAs a result, you have what is called a “reversal breakout”. You catch on quick'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Mr. Elliott showed that a trending market moves in what he calls a 5-3 wave pattern.

The first 5-wave pattern is called impulse waves.

The last 3-wave pattern is called corrective waves.

In this pattern, Waves 1, 3, 5 are motive, meaning they go along with the overall trend, while Waves 2 and 4 are corrective.

Do not confuse Waves 2 and 4 with the ABC corrective pattern (discussed in the next section) though!
Let’s first take a look at the 5-wave impulse pattern. It’s easier if you see it as a picture:'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Wave 1:-
The Currency makes its initial move upwards.

This is usually caused by a relatively small number of people sudden feel price is cheap it is right time to buy .



Wave'}, ' 2 :- At this point, enough people who were in the original wave consider the coin overvalued and take profits.

This causes the coin to go down.


Wave 3 :- This is usually the longest and strongest wave. The Coin has caught the attention of the mass public.

Wave 4 :- Traders take profits because the stock is considered expensive again.

Wave 5 :- This is the point that most people get on the coin and is most driven by hysteria']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'One thing that you also need to know about the Elliott Wave Theory is that one of the three impulse waves
 (1, 3, or 5) will always be “extended.”

Simply put, there will always be one wave that is longer than the other two, regardless of degree.

According to Elliott, it is usually the fifth wave which is extended.'}, '']

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#Corrective'}, ' Wave in Elliot wave theory  I m sharing some few point in next post stay tuned with us..']

Corrective Wave in Bullish Trend.The 5-wave trends are then corrected and reversed by 3-wave countertrends.

Letters are used instead of numbers to track the correction.
we’ve been using a bull market as my primary example doesn’t mean the Elliott Wave Theory doesn’t work on bear markets.The same 5-3 wave pattern can look like this.
Let’s take a look at these three formations. The examples below apply to uptrends, but you can just invert them if you’re dealing with a downtrend.

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Zig-zag formations are very steep moves in price that goes against the predominant trend.
   Wave B is typically shortest in length compared to Waves A and C.
These zig-zag patterns can happen twice or even thrice in a correction (2 to 3 zig-zag patterns linked together).

Like with all waves, each of the waves in zig-zag patterns could be broken up into 5-wave patterns.'}]

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Flat formations are simple sideways corrective waves.

In flats, the lengths of the waves are GENERALLY equal in length, with wave B reversing wave A’s move and wave C undoing wave B’s move.

We say generally because wave B can sometimes go beyond the beginning of wave A.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Triangle formations are corrective patterns that are bound by either converging or diverging trend lines.\n\nTriangles are made up of 5-waves that move against the trend in a sideways fashion. These triangles can be symmetrical, descending, ascending, or expanding.'}]

There are THREE cardinal “cannot-be-broken” rules in labeling waves.

So, before you jump right into applying the Elliott Wave Theory to your trading, you must take note of the rules below.👇👇👇👇👇👇👇👇👇👇👇👇

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'There are the guidelines that help you correctly label waves.

Unlike the three cardinal rules, these guidelines can be broken. Here they are:

Sometimes, Wave 5 does not move beyond the end of wave 3. This is called truncation.

Wave 5, more often than not, goes beyond or “breaks through” the trend line drawn off Wave 3 parallel to a trend line connecting the start of Waves 3 and 5.

Wave 3 tends to be very long, sharp and extended.

Waves 2 and 4 frequently bounce off Fibonacci retracement levels.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'STOP LOSS HUNT : Generally it is done by Big palyers / Institutions / Whales etc. To take a big position on a particular place, They First buy on Resistance or sell on Support and break the level, Due to that many Retailers change its direction to Trade as they turned bullish or Bearish as per Breakout so They place ther Buy Sell Order which Whales use to place their orders. 

Explantion through chart above : Consider a Whale want to short from $8000+ a big amount. They know if they will place 1000 BTC (just for example) order at $8000. for that there should be buy orders of 1000 BTC volume at $8000+. Otherwise their Market order will swallow all orders below $8000 Price upto 1000 BTC volume, which can be upto $7800 because nobody would like to buy just below Resistance. Hence they will get entry at average price near $7900. So they want 1000 BTC must be in long above $8000 to keep their Entry above $8000 on average. So they broke Resistance through a buy of 10-20% of their amount like with 200 BTC and many retailer Turned  Bullish and longed from $8000+ (as many of them have set it though Bots) Now Retailers who shorted from $8000 they had stop loss in between ($8060-$8150) and also those who longed from $8050 they all will be considered as buy orders where that whale will short 1000 BTC and swallow their Buy orders by keeping their average entry Price above $8000. Hope you all got it.

in the chart of 30 mins Time frame you can see Price dropped from $8130 to $7870 very quickly this is Bcz any Whale shorted a big amount from there.

Trading has taught me a lot of things...

1: This is not a Get Rich Quickly scheme. It requires patience and dedication.

2: Try to learn as much as possible. But also stick to one strategy. 

3: Once you graps the concept and understand the psychological side of Trading, you are half way through. 

4: After your analysis, trust what you predict and also use proper Risk Management.

5: Other people's opinions tend to confuse even more. Don't let other people have the final say about your analysis. We don't trade the same. Trust Your Analysis Trader. 

6: Most of the things, you learn them when you're alone. You do get information there and there that may help you trade better, but you are the driver of your own account.

After 6 Months : $1610 + $16105 = $17715
After 7 Months : $1771 + $17715 = $19486
After 8 Months : $1948 + $19486 = $21434
After 9 Months : $2143 + $21434 = $23577
After 10 Months : $2357 + $23577 = $25934
After 11 Months : $2593 + $25934 = $28527
After 12 Months : $2852 + $28527 = $31379

So with only making 5R consistently you can do 3X Your balance in 1 Year. Which You can turn 9X in second Year. So it takes only 2 years to convert $10000 to $100K with consistency and One Year for $30K.

Understand Risk Management, Plan according to your Capability. This one month can be 1 week or 2 weeks as per your Trading Style. Good Luck'}, ' 👍🏻

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Trading is difficult because no method, system, or style works in all markets, all the time.

Profitability is primarily a result of losing small when you are wrong, and maximizing profits when you are right. Knowing and trading your edge is the best path to profits. Here is who makes (or loses) money in different types of markets:

Trend followers make money when a strong market trend persists for months. They lose money when markets give false signals and reverse and stop them out.
Swing traders lose money when support and resistance do not hold.
Day traders lose money in markets that fail to move in one direction intraday, and instead move fast and erratically.
Option premium sellers get hurt in sharply trending markets when they sell spreads, or naked options.
Option buyers get hurt in markets that move against their options, don’t trend enough, or that don’t move enough before their expiration.
Momentum traders lose money in markets that are range-bound or tend to reverse after break outs.
Investors lose money in bear markets.
Buy and holders lose money in bear markets.
Perma-Bulls lose money in bear markets.
Perma-Bears lose money in bull markets.
Fundamentalists lose money in any market that doesn’t conform to their analysis of what should happen.
Traders that trade too big of position size blow up eventually in any market environment.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Here are the first and most important lessons that a new trader needs to understand. All traders have to learn these lessons either the easy way and learn them from others or they can learn them the hard way by losing their own money. 

1. Do not start trading until you have a quantified trading system with and edge. 
2. Trade your system with consistency and discipline over the long term with perseverance. 
3. Every trade should have a good risk/reward ratio on entry. Your stop loss versus your profit target should give you the chance to make $300 for every $100 you risk. 
4. Always go in the direction of least resistance for your trade. Stay on the side of the trend for your timeframe. 
5. Trade position sizes to bring your risk of ruin down to close to zero. 
Profitable trading is built on the principles of math and psychology that work over the long term not opinions, predictions, ego, and luck. 

“If you diversify, control your risk, and go with the trend, it just has to work.”'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'What is a Trading Plan?

Be your own trader.

In other words: Don’t follow someone else’s trading advice blindly!

Just because someone may be doing well with their method, it doesn’t mean it will work for you.
We’re all in different situations in life, and we all have different market views, thought processes, risk tolerance levels, and market experience.



Developing a Trading Plan and sticking to it are the two main ingredients of trading discipline.

But trading discipline isn’t enough. Even solid trading discipline isn’t enough.
It has to be rock solid discipline.

We repeat: rock solid.

Like a brick wall.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'A trading plan defines what is supposed to be done, why, when, and how.

 It covers your trader personality, personal expectations, risk management rules, and trading system(s).


When followed, a trading plan will help limit trading mistakes and minimize your losses.

After all, “If you fail to plan, then you’ve already planned to fail.”

A trading plan removes any bad decision making in the heat of the moment.

Your emotions can consume you when money is on the line, causing you to make irrational decisions. You don’t want that to happen.
The best way to prevent it from happening is to minimize (notice we did not say eliminate) thinking by having a plan for every potential market action.


With the right  trading plan, every action is spelled out, so that in the heat of the moment you don’t have to make any rash decisions.

You just simply stick to your trading plan.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'The Difference Between a Trading Plan and a Trading System'}, '

', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'A trading system describes how you will enter and exit trades.

A trading system is PART of your trading plan but is just one of several important parts, i.e., analysis, executions, risk management, etc. 

Since market conditions are always changing, a good trader will usually have two or more trading systems in his or her trading plan.

Trading systems will be covered more in-depth later on in the lesson, but we thought that it was important to point out the difference between the two upfront to avoid any confusion.





A trading plan will make trading simpler than it would be if you traded without one.

Think of when you use a GPS device.

You enter where you want to go. It then figures out where you currently are and then shows you how to get to where you want to go.

You’re able to constantly check on your GPS to see if you’re still on the right track.

When you make a wrong turn, it knows to make adjustments, and it points you back in the right direction.

A trading plan is your trading GPS. It will show you where you currently are as a trader and help you get to your destination: consistent profitability.


Without a trading plan, this would be nearly impossible. Instead, you’d be a “cowboy trader“, shooting from the hip, trading by the seat of your pants, relying on your gut, guesses or signals from strangers.

That ain’t trading – that’s gambling!'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'A bearish pennant is formed during a steep, almost vertical, downtrend.

After that sharp drop in price, some sellers close their positions while other sellers decide to join the trend, making the price consolidate for a bit.


As soon as enough sellers jump in, the price breaks below the bottom of the pennant and continues to move down.



As you can see, the drop resumed after the price made a breakout to the bottom.

To trade this chart pattern, we’d put a short order at the bottom of the pennant with a stop loss above the pennant.

That way, we’d be out of the trade right away in case the breakdown was a fake out.
Unlike the other chart patterns wherein the size of the next move is approximately the height of the formation, pennants signal much stronger moves.

Usually, the height of the earlier move (also known as the mast) is used to estimate the size of the breakout move.'}, '']

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'What affects gold prices?


Demand and Supply:
When there is a rise in demand for gold, the price increases, and vice versa. Gold is one commodity that is continuously in demand. Demand and supply play a major role in pricing of gold.

Inflation:
As gold prices react to inflation, Indians prefer to invest in gold. When inflation rises the currency values go down. Thus, people tend to hold money in the form of gold. When inflation lasts high for a long period of time, gold acts as a hedging tool against inflationary conditions. As the value of currency keeps fluctuating, gold value is considered stable in the long run.

Central Bank of India:
The central bank’s decision to buy or sell gold can affect the price due to the sheer volume of transactions.


Interest rates:
Both gold and interest rates have an inverse relationship. When the interest rates increase, people sell off their gold and use the money to earn high interest. When the interest rates decrease, people buy more gold resulting in increase in demand.

Monsoon:
Rural demand plays an important role in demand for gold; the maximum purchase of gold in India is done from rural market. Good monsoon results in good harvest and the amount earned is used to invest in gold which is used in rainy season as in poor monsoon gold acts as a safe haven.

Import Duty:
As gold is not produced in India, it is important for consumption from other countries and import duty plays a crucial role in price fluctuations.



Government Reserves:
The government holds reserves of gold. When RBI starts to buy greater quantity than it sells, the price increases as it will result in insufficient supply of gold and vice versa.


Protection from volatility or uncertainty:
To protect from uncertainties, people wish to invest or buy gold as it is a safe commodity.


Correlation with other assets:
Gold is a highly effective portfolio diversification because of its low negative correlation with all the major asset classes. When shares of companies fall there is an inverse relationship shown between gold and equities.

Geopolitical factors:
During geopolitical turmoil, gold usually does well. During crises various asset classes have a negative impact but there is a positive impact towards gold as it acts as a safe haven for parking of funds.'}, '']

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#NOTE'}, ' : ', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Many Times I get a question, what time Particular TF Candle will close, However its basic Maths, and best way to find its answer on Google, But still I am making it simple for you. as following : 

👉🏻 First learn how to convert UTC Price to your Time Zone. (For Example : My TZ is +5:30 GMT , That means my TZ is ahead of UTC TZ by 5 hrs and 30 minutes, if MY TZ had Minus Sign, that meant I am behind UTC TZ.)

👉🏻 Daily Candle Closes at 23:59:59 UTC and opens at 00:00 UTC. So you just have to change it to your TZ and remember that time as Daily open and close Time. (Ex : For My TZ, Daily Candle opens at 5:30 AM).

👉🏻 There are 24 hrs in a day, so 4Hr Candle closes after every 4 Hrs starting from 00:00 UTC.  hope you can add that 6 times a day.

👉🏻 There are 24 hrs in a day, so 1Hr Candle closes after every 1 Hr starting from 00:00 UTC.

I think this should clear the doubts.
'}, '*TF : Time Frame
*TZ : time Zone']

Initial Balance :  $10000 USD
After 1 Month  : $1000 + $10000 = $11000
After 2 Months : $1100 + $11000 = $12100
After 3 Months : $1210 + $12100 = $13310
After 4 Months : $1331 + $13310 = $14641
After 5 Months : $1464 + $14641 = $16105
After 6 Months : $1610 + $16105 = $17715
After 7 Months : $1771 + $17715 = $19486
After 8 Months : $1948 + $19486 = $21434
After 9 Months : $2143 + $21434 = $23577
After 10 Months : $2357 + $23577 = $25934
After 11 Months : $2593 + $25934 = $28527
After 12 Months : $2852 + $28527 = $31379

So with only making 5R consistently you can do 3X Your balance in 1 Year. Which You can turn 9X in second Year. So it takes only 2 years to convert $10000 to $100K with consistency and One Year for $30K.

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#Tips'}, ' ', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'For Leverage and Position Size : 

Simple explanation of leverage : 

Lets Assume a Trade set up is like this =

'}, {'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#XYZ'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': " Coin
BUY : 5000
SELL : 7000
STOP : 4900

Assume we have $10000 USD in wallet.

So we know SL is 4900, That's equal to 2%

I am considering Risk for this trade : 3%

So position size = (10000 * 3) / 2 = 15000 USD

But we have only 10000 in wallet so how would one take a position size of 15000 USD.

Solution : we will use leverage.

Because we don't want to put all our money in one trade, And we don't have 15000 USD as well.


Formula to find minimum required leverage = Position size / wallet Balance.

 "}, {'type': 'phone', 'text': '15000/10000'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': ' = 1.5

Hence we need minimum 1.5x leverage to take this position. 

Now bcz SL is 2% so maximum possible leverage for this trade to avoid liquidation , 

Formula = 100 / SL% = 100/2 = 50

So maximum leverage for this trade = 50x

Now you can use any leverage between 1.5x to 50x to make your position size 15000 USD and it will give you same profit.

Leverage Example : I use 5x , It will take only 3000 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (5*3000). And now I can take another trades with remaining 7000 USD.

if I use 10x, It will take only 1500 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (10*1500). And now I can take another trades with remaining 8500 USD.

'}, {'type': 'italic', 'text': "NOTE : All these calculations exclude Exchange fees and Liquidation fee. Hence if your maximum leverage is 50x, I will not recommend using more than 40x. I personally never take leverage more than half of the maximum limit. So if 50x leverage is possible for trade, I will never use more than 25x or 20x. Because on crypto exchanges volatility is very high and you all know about scam wicks, which can liquidate you or even you may have to pay high slippage. So always avoid high leverage and even trade with full wallet balance on lowest possible leverage in case you don't need funds for other trades."}]

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#Tips'}, ' ', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'For Leverage and Position Size : 

Simple explanation of leverage : 

Lets Assume a Trade set up is like this =

'}, {'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#XYZ'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': " Coin
BUY : 5000
SELL : 7000
STOP : 4900

Assume we have $10000 USD in wallet.

So we know SL is 4900, That's equal to 2%

I am considering Risk for this trade : 3%

So position size = (10000 * 3) / 2 = 15000 USD

But we have only 10000 in wallet so how would one take a position size of 15000 USD.

Solution : we will use leverage.

Because we don't want to put all our money in one trade, And we don't have 15000 USD as well.


Formula to find minimum required leverage = Position size / wallet Balance.

 "}, {'type': 'phone', 'text': '15000/10000'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': ' = 1.5

Hence we need minimum 1.5x leverage to take this position. 

Now bcz SL is 2% so maximum possible leverage for this trade to avoid liquidation , 

Formula = 100 / SL% = 100/2 = 50

So maximum leverage for this trade = 50x

Now you can use any leverage between 1.5x to 50x to make your position size 15000 USD and it will give you same profit.

Leverage Example : I use 5x , It will take only 3000 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (5*3000). And now I can take another trades with remaining 7000 USD.

if I use 10x, It will take only 1500 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (10*1500). And now I can take another trades with remaining 8500 USD.

'}, {'type': 'italic', 'text': "NOTE : All these calculations exclude Exchange fees and Liquidation fee. Hence if your maximum leverage is 50x, I will not recommend using more than 40x. I personally never take leverage more than half of the maximum limit. So if 50x leverage is possible for trade, I will never use more than 25x or 20x. Because on crypto exchanges volatility is very high and you all know about scam wicks, which can liquidate you or even you may have to pay high slippage. So always avoid high leverage and even trade with full wallet balance on lowest possible leverage in case you don't need funds for other trades."}]

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#Tips'}, ' ', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'For Leverage and Position Size : 

Simple explanation of leverage : 

Lets Assume a Trade set up is like this =

'}, {'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#XYZ'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': " Coin
BUY : 5000
SELL : 7000
STOP : 4900

Assume we have $10000 USD in wallet.

So we know SL is 4900, That's equal to 2%

I am considering Risk for this trade : 3%

So position size = (10000 * 3) / 2 = 15000 USD

But we have only 10000 in wallet so how would one take a position size of 15000 USD.

Solution : we will use leverage.

Because we don't want to put all our money in one trade, And we don't have 15000 USD as well.


Formula to find minimum required leverage = Position size / wallet Balance.

 "}, {'type': 'phone', 'text': '15000/10000'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': ' = 1.5

Hence we need minimum 1.5x leverage to take this position. 

Now bcz SL is 2% so maximum possible leverage for this trade to avoid liquidation , 

Formula = 100 / SL% = 100/2 = 50

So maximum leverage for this trade = 50x

Now you can use any leverage between 1.5x to 50x to make your position size 15000 USD and it will give you same profit.

Leverage Example : I use 5x , It will take only 3000 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (5*3000). And now I can take another trades with remaining 7000 USD.

if I use 10x, It will take only 1500 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (10*1500). And now I can take another trades with remaining 8500 USD.

'}, {'type': 'italic', 'text': "NOTE : All these calculations exclude Exchange fees and Liquidation fee. Hence if your maximum leverage is 50x, I will not recommend using more than 40x. I personally never take leverage more than half of the maximum limit. So if 50x leverage is possible for trade, I will never use more than 25x or 20x. Because on crypto exchanges volatility is very high and you all know about scam wicks, which can liquidate you or even you may have to pay high slippage. So always avoid high leverage and even trade with full wallet balance on lowest possible leverage in case you don't need funds for other trades."}]

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#Tips'}, ' ', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'For Leverage and Position Size : 

Simple explanation of leverage : 

Lets Assume a Trade set up is like this =

'}, {'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#XYZ'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': " Coin
BUY : 5000
SELL : 7000
STOP : 4900

Assume we have $10000 USD in wallet.

So we know SL is 4900, That's equal to 2%

I am considering Risk for this trade : 3%

So position size = (10000 * 3) / 2 = 15000 USD

But we have only 10000 in wallet so how would one take a position size of 15000 USD.

Solution : we will use leverage.

Because we don't want to put all our money in one trade, And we don't have 15000 USD as well.


Formula to find minimum required leverage = Position size / wallet Balance.

 "}, {'type': 'phone', 'text': '15000/10000'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': ' = 1.5

Hence we need minimum 1.5x leverage to take this position. 

Now bcz SL is 2% so maximum possible leverage for this trade to avoid liquidation , 

Formula = 100 / SL% = 100/2 = 50

So maximum leverage for this trade = 50x

Now you can use any leverage between 1.5x to 50x to make your position size 15000 USD and it will give you same profit.

Leverage Example : I use 5x , It will take only 3000 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (5*3000). And now I can take another trades with remaining 7000 USD.

if I use 10x, It will take only 1500 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (10*1500). And now I can take another trades with remaining 8500 USD.

'}, {'type': 'italic', 'text': "NOTE : All these calculations exclude Exchange fees and Liquidation fee. Hence if your maximum leverage is 50x, I will not recommend using more than 40x. I personally never take leverage more than half of the maximum limit. So if 50x leverage is possible for trade, I will never use more than 25x or 20x. Because on crypto exchanges volatility is very high and you all know about scam wicks, which can liquidate you or even you may have to pay high slippage. So always avoid high leverage and even trade with full wallet balance on lowest possible leverage in case you don't need funds for other trades."}]

[{'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#Tips'}, ' ', {'type': 'bold', 'text': 'For Leverage and Position Size : 

Simple explanation of leverage : 

Lets Assume a Trade set up is like this =

'}, {'type': 'hashtag', 'text': '#XYZ'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': " Coin
BUY : 5000
SELL : 7000
STOP : 4900

Assume we have $10000 USD in wallet.

So we know SL is 4900, That's equal to 2%

I am considering Risk for this trade : 3%

So position size = (10000 * 3) / 2 = 15000 USD

But we have only 10000 in wallet so how would one take a position size of 15000 USD.

Solution : we will use leverage.

Because we don't want to put all our money in one trade, And we don't have 15000 USD as well.


Formula to find minimum required leverage = Position size / wallet Balance.

 "}, {'type': 'phone', 'text': '15000/10000'}, {'type': 'bold', 'text': ' = 1.5

Hence we need minimum 1.5x leverage to take this position. 

Now bcz SL is 2% so maximum possible leverage for this trade to avoid liquidation , 

Formula = 100 / SL% = 100/2 = 50

So maximum leverage for this trade = 50x

Now you can use any leverage between 1.5x to 50x to make your position size 15000 USD and it will give you same profit.

Leverage Example : I use 5x , It will take only 3000 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (5*3000). And now I can take another trades with remaining 7000 USD.

if I use 10x, It will take only 1500 USD from my wallet and I will be able to open a position of 15000 USD (10*1500). And now I can take another trades with remaining 8500 USD.

'}, {'type': 'italic', 'text': "NOTE : All these calculations exclude Exchange fees and Liquidation fee. Hence if your maximum leverage is 50x, I will not recommend using more than 40x. I personally never take leverage more than half of the maximum limit. So if 50x leverage is possible for trade, I will never use more than 25x or 20x. Because on crypto exchanges volatility is very high and you all know about scam wicks, which can liquidate you or even you may have to pay high slippage. So always avoid high leverage and even trade with full wallet balance on lowest possible leverage in case you don't need funds for other trades."}]

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Reminder !!!

❗️❗️Important Rules to follow trading leverage / future contracts for scalps and swings. ❗️❗️

👉🏻Avoid using Cross leverage unless you are very pro with using it, Use Isolated Leverage to manage unsystematic risk (which is not in your hands) and make it systematic by doing this.

👉🏻 Don't use high leverage than needed.  if your position Size is good with 3X leverage , you don't have to use 15x. Keep leverage below 10x in alts specially.

👉🏻 If you understand position size and leverage very well, only then use high leverage but still try to keep liquidation price at least 2x distant from Stop loss.

👉🏻 Never leave your trade open without Stop Loss and Entry. In any case if you have not placed Target is okay, But don't leave trade open without Stop loss, Even if you wanna take additional risk to avoid stop hunt as per your analysis, still keep it distant but don't leave trade without Stop loss. I would also suggest placing Target Order as well.
(As it is said, "Your Stop loss should be in System not mind.")

👉🏻 Always pay the trader. we as analyst fortunate enough to find out high probabilities of Price movement direction from one level to another but what after another level is more a speculation than probability. And we want you to understand this that we are Research Analyst not Astrologer, that can exactly tell what will happen after first target, second target of price drops back to Entry, So Always book at least 40-60% on First Target, We usually try to keep First target above +1.5R or higher. That's enough for you to be profitable with a 60% win rate, if you are disciplined.

👉🏻 Above this all make a trading plan , rules and conditions for your own trading. When will you enter a trade, on what type of signals, on what type of set ups, what should be minimum RRR for the trade, where would you book profit, how much would you risk and a lot from your own sense, and just execute that. Give yourself at least 100 Trades or 3 months and you will see a big change in your portfolio.

Read all this and good Luck. Feel Free to DM for any help.'}, '  ', {'type': 'mention', 'text': '@wowitstony'}, '']

Taproot is a soft fork that improves Bitcoin’s scripts to increase privacy and improve upon other factors related to complex transactions. Transactions on the Bitcoin network can use various features that make them more complex, including timelock releases, multi-signature requirements, and others.

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'So The first thing I notice on price/chart while Trading is  "Target" and you should always do the same, If you don\'t know where price is going ? what is its objective and why its going there ? there is no meaning of planning a trade.\n\nFor Example : If BTC price is $36K now, So first thing I want to ask myself where its going ? That\'s a target. Now I check my invalidation or stop loss, the level if it loose I would expect it would go opposite and then you think about an Entry, what would be the best entry for a good RRR for this trade. Its easy and we need to keep our focus this way.'}]

[{'type': 'bold', 'text': 'Moving Average Explanations : These MAs are most common to be used as they provide the data of : 

MA 5-7 : Weekly data
MA 11 : Bi-weekly data
MA 20-21 : Monthly data
MA 50-60 : Quarterly data
MA 100 (usually used with combination of MA 50) : 1 STND deviation of MA 50
MA 200 : 40 weeks data (good enough to define long term trend)
MA 365 : Yearly data

Except that all other numbers of MAs used by individuals are based on their personal strategies, These are the MAs which have higher significance across the Technical Analysts.'}]
